# PLATFORM_CAPABILITY_MATRIX.md тАФ Feature Comparison Across Platforms

**Generated by** `strategy/gen_platform_docs.py` тАФ the merged, cross-platform successor to
`check_platform_status.py -GenerateMatrix`. **Single source:** `strategy/PLATFORM_DATA.json`
(C.R.A.S.H. capability grids + the installer `_platform_capabilities.json` + the engine integration
tier, unified). **34 platforms** (was 23 in the PowerShell version тАФ extended to the full set).

Legend: тЬЕ verified ┬╖ тЪая╕П partial ┬╖ тЭМ not supported ┬╖ тЭУ untested.

---

**Capability columns** (unchanged from the canonical matrix) + the engine dimension:

| Column | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Hooks | Native lifecycle hook system + gald3r hook wiring |
| Rules | Persistent always-apply rules / memory injection |
| Skills | `g-skl-*/SKILL.md` discovery + invocation |
| Commands | `@g-*` slash commands / workflow equivalents |
| MCP | Model Context Protocol server support |
| **Engine tier** | how the bundled gald3r engine reaches the platform тАФ **L2 MCP** (the 37 tools, 32/34 platforms), **L1 CLI** (`uv run тАж gald3r`), **L0 files-only** (`SKILL.full.md` fallback) |
| Rules ext | per-platform rule file extension (`.md` / `.mdc` / single-file), from `_platform_capabilities.json` |

The engine tier is the new column: even where a native capability is тЭМ/тЪая╕П, the engine supplies that
behavior via MCP/CLI on 32/34 platforms тАФ so the *effective* readiness is higher than the
native columns alone. See `COMBINED_READINESS.md` for the per-platform lift.
